text: In this work, we propose a novel framework, Adaptive Recursive Neural Network (AdaRNN) for the task of target-dependent sentiment classification on Twitter. Most models consider the overall sentiment of a tweet for a target, and fail to model how word-level sentiments adapt to a particular target by taking into account local context and syntactic information. AdaRNN leverages the strengths of multi-composition functions to capture the sentiment flow into a target by modeling a probability distribution over these composition functions to dynamically chose which functions to use given the linguistic patterns present in the input, such as negations and amplification. We show that AdaRNN outperforms all the existing baselines on this task. Moreover, we introduce a new Twitter dataset for target-dependent sentiment classification which is manually labeled and released for research purposes.
